problem_summary: Sequential BOED on y = a*xi + b + Normal(0, sigma_obs) with vague Gaussian priors on slope and intercept. Pyro backend (explicit simulator).

latent_spec.notes: Slope a and intercept b; vague Normal(0, 3) priors by default.

observation_spec.notes: Single scalar observation per round; additive Gaussian noise with sigma=0.1.

design_variables.name: xi
design_variables.description: Design coordinate; BOED picks the most informative xi per round.

hints.prior_notes: Intentionally vague Gaussian priors — no prior elicitation. The point is to demonstrate the sequential loop, not the prior.

candidates.name: pyro_linreg
candidates.description: Explicit Pyro model with posterior_eig and an amortised bivariate-normal posterior guide over (a, b) | y. Exact variational family for this linear-Gaussian model, so the posterior EIG estimator is tight in the asymptotic limit.

metadata.fallback_path: examples.cases.linreg_sequential.model:GaussianLinearModel — numpy conjugate closed-form, used when pyro-ppl is not installed.
